text: Concurrent strength and endurance training does not reduce the magnitude of VO2max improvement compared to endurance training alone.

Concurrent strength and endurance training reduces the capacity to develop maximal strength compared to strength training alone, with strength gains plateauing around week 7 and declining in weeks 9-10.
specific_value: strength plateau at ~7 weeks, decline at weeks 9-10

text: Endurance training alone (40 min/day, 6 days/week) does not produce appreciable gains in leg strength.
Strength training alone (30-40 min/day, 5 days/week) does not increase VO2max.
The interference effect in concurrent training is directional: endurance capacity is preserved but strength development is impaired, particularly after 7 weeks of combined training.

Strength gains during concurrent training follow a similar trajectory to strength-only training for the first 7 weeks before diverging, suggesting the interference effect is cumulative rather than immediate.
